cot_prefix: Okay, let's try to figure this out. The problem says that John is correct about today's date being 6/19/2019. We need to find the date one year ago from today, in MM/DD/YYYY format. The options are given, so maybe we can check each one. First, if today is June 19, 2019, then one year ago would be June 19, 2018. But wait, I need to make sure about leap years or something? Let me think. June 19, 2018... 2016 was a leap year, 2020 is a leap year. So 2018 isn't a leap year. But since June is after February, the leap year wouldn't affect the calculation here. So subtracting one year from 6/19/2019 should be 6/19/2018. Let me check the options. Looking at the options, option A is 06/19/2018, which matches that. But wait, the options also have option F as 06/18/2018. But why would that be an option? Because maybe someone might think to subtract a day if there's a leap year? But no, since 2018 isn't a leap year. Also, June 19 minus one year is straightforward. So the correct answer should be option A. Let me double-check the other options. Option B is May 22, which doesn't make sense. Options
